center.name: James Chapin
center.summary: James Chapin是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联4种鸟类，包括剛果孔雀、Ituri batis、Forest swallow等4种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The forest swallow (Atronanus fuliginosus) is a little-known species of swallow in the family Hirundinidae, found in Cameroon, Republic of the Congo, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon, and Nigeria

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Ituri batis or Chapin's batis (Batis ituriensis) is a species of bird in the wattle-eye family, Platysteiridae which is found in the humid forests of eastern central Africa

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |year=2022 |title=Afropavo congensis |volume=2022 |article-number=e.T22679430A208189646 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2022-1.RLTS.T22679430A208189646.en |access-date=27 November 2025}} | genus = Afropavo | parent_authority = Chapin, 1936 | display_parents = 2 | species = congensis | authority = Chapin, 1936 }} The Congo peafowl (Afropavo congensis), also known as the African peafowl or mbulu by the Bakôngo, is a species native to the Congo Basin.{{cite book |last1=Dowsett |first1=R

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The broad-tailed paradise whydah (Vidua obtusa) is a species of bird in the family Viduidae. It is found in woodland and acacia savanna habitat in Sub-Saharan Africa from Angola to Uganda, Tanzania and Mozambique
